title: helianthus resinosus
intro: Helianthus resinosus, also known as the resinous sunflower, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its large, daisy-like flowers and sticky, resinous leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The resinous sunflower is said to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. The plant's leaves and stems are often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and topical salves, which can be used to soothe skin conditions, reduce pain, and promote overall well-being.
